exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained and clear focus on the prescribed title, repeatedly returning to the central question of how knowledge affects wonder.
Effectively integrates relevant examples from the natural sciences (DNA, CRISPR, microbiology) and the arts (Jackson Pollock, Hemingway) to support the two main viewpoints.
Arguments are generally coherent and organized around contrasting positions, demonstrating logical progression.
Acknowledges multiple perspectives (separation of art and artist, scientific certainty versus ongoing curiosity), showing awareness of alternative points of view.
weaknesses: Analysis of key TOK concepts (evidence, methodology, personal/cultural perspectives) remains implicit and superficial, limiting depth of evaluation.
Alternative perspectives and counterclaims are noted only briefly, without sustained critical weighing of their relative strengths or implications.
Definitions (e.g., âwonderâ) and references (citations, quotations) are under-developed or inconsistently applied, detracting from conceptual clarity and scholarly rigor.
Conclusion summarizes discussion but lacks a clear, evaluative judgement or final stance on the prescribed title to reinforce the essayâs overall argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band: it sustains focus on the title, links effectively to areas of knowledge with appropriate and explained examples, and demonstrates awareness of different viewpoints. However, it does not probe key underlying assumptions, nor does it engage in the depth of evaluation required for higher bands. Weaknesses in conceptual definitions, critical engagement with counterclaims, and a conclusive evaluative stance limit its overall impact. A score of 6 appropriately reflects the strong organization and supporting examples, while accounting for the noted gaps in analytical rigor and TOK conceptual development.

annotations.comment: The transition to Jackson Pollock and fractals is intriguing, but the essay should explain what âfractal characteristicsâ entail and how they relate to viewersâ aesthetic wonder.
The student notes that emotional impact persists despite technical knowledge, yet a TOK analysis should explore how personal and cultural perspectives mediate this response.
Consider including an alternative perspective: some art historians argue that technical knowledge can increase wonder by revealing hidden complexity. Evaluating this view would strengthen balance.
The link between Pollockâs historical context and wonder is briefly mentioned; deepen this by analyzing how shifts in artistic norms alter collective appreciation and wonder.
The microbiology example from Ed Yong is vivid and effectively connects the emergence of new questions to how knowledge can enhance wonder.
The definition of âwonderâ is broad and repeats synonyms; refine to an operational definition and explicitly link it to TOK ways of knowing to strengthen conceptual clarity.
The CRISPR example illustrates application but misses discussion of its ethical implications and how those uncertainties shape wonderâan essential TOK perspective.
Additional sources are listed separately here, diluting coherence. Consolidate extended references into the main Works Cited list.
The opening page clearly states the prescribed title and session, but including a concise thesis preview here would guide the readerâs expectations from the outset.
While the introduction outlines the essayâs scope, the thesis statement emerges late. A succinct thesis early in this paragraph would sharpen the essayâs argumentative focus.

The Watson and Crick example is descriptive but lacks analysis of how their methodological choices influenced the sense of wonder. Use TOK terminology (evidence, methodology) to deepen the evaluation.
When defining heredity, the parenthetical reference to Cambridge Dictionary interrupts flow. Integrate definitions smoothly and relate them directly to the knowledge question.
The essay notes CRISPRâs success but does not consider unforeseen consequences or uncertainties, which would illustrate how incomplete knowledge fuels further wonder.
The citation â(BBC)â is vague. Provide author and date or a fuller reference here to enhance source credibility and traceability.
The discussion paraphrases Cantore but does not critically evaluate his claim that wonder is the starting point for science. Offer counterclaims or question his assumptions.
The Works Cited heading is in place, but entries should be grouped alphabetically and formatted consistently for professionalism.
Several in-text citations (e.g., Cambridge Dictionary, Nature) are missing from this bibliography. Ensure all referenced works appear here to maintain academic integrity.
The term âmystical wonderâ appears without clarification; specify what aspect of wonder is contrasted to intellectual wonder to avoid ambiguity.
The discussion cites Lumen Learning on art interpretation but the three components are not specified. Briefly outlining them would clarify how technical knowledge fits into interpretation.
The Oscar Wilde quotation is incomplete and lacks precise reference details (year and work); ensure full quotation and citation to maintain scholarly rigour.
The Hemingway case explores art/artist separation well, but omits analysis of the personal knowledge frameworks that enable this detachmentâintegrate WOKs to deepen insight.
The concluding statements summarize key points but stop short of an evaluative judgment on the prescribed title. Assert a reasoned final stance to reinforce the conclusion.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (varying date formats, missing italics). Apply a single citation style consistently across entries.
The final web-source entries omit publication dates and publishers. Include full publication information to strengthen source reliability.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
